findings.title: Parser reports dscli and other decoy processes as workers
findings.impact: parse_worker_lines() defines _WORKER_BIN_RE but never uses it before appending a match, so any process whose command line contains --worker_address is returned as a running worker. A concurrent dscli stop --worker_address=... or another helper process will be printed by dscli status with its PID and address, producing a wrong status result and contradicting the documented worker-only contract.
findings.fix: Before extracting/appending the address, require _WORKER_BIN_RE.search(cmdline); skip the line when the actual datasystem_worker executable token is absent.
